title: ternatea bracteata
intro: Ternatea bracteata, also known as the Australian Hops or Hops Bush, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It is a shrub-like plant with woody stems and white or cream-colored flowers that have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Ternatea bracteata include its ability to act as a sedative, anxiolytic, and antispasmodic, making it useful for treating anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which is often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality.
